response.justification: Sarah Smith is a capable full-stack and web-focused engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but she fails the most critical hard requirements for this role. She has no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever (the job requires native-level Japanese), no embedded networking or LAN-switch security experience, no meaningful C/UNIX systems programming background beyond a thesis project and a brief stint at NI, and no Linux kernel development or modification experience. Her entire career arc — web platforms, direct mail automation, GIS tooling, TypeScript/Node.js — is orthogonal to the embedded firmware and network security domain this role requires.
strengths: 15+ years of consistent software delivery across diverse domains demonstrates solid engineering fundamentals
Some C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows CVI, wave field synthesis thesis) shows at least passing familiarity with lower-level languages
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and conference talks indicate a collaborative, quality-focused practitioner
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — this is a stated hard requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF are entirely absent from the resume)
No Linux kernel development or modification experience; all Linux usage appears to be at the application/infrastructure layer, not kernel level

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
